title: Iran's Women Soccer Players Return Home from Australia
summary: Three members of Iran's women's soccer team have decided to go back to Iran instead of staying in Australia as refugees. They were part of a group of seven players and staff who had chosen to stay in Australia after a soccer tournament. Now, only three of them remain in Australia. The Australian government said the players were given chances to talk about their options before leaving. The team came to Australia for a soccer competition, and some players did not sing Iran's national anthem, which made people worry about their safety in Iran. Iranian groups and the U.S. president asked Australia to help the women. An Iranian news agency said the players' return was a failure for America and Australia.

background_read: This article is about women soccer players from Iran who were in Australia for a tournament. Some players chose to stay in Australia as refugees because they might not be safe in Iran. Refugees are people who leave their home country to find safety elsewhere. In sports, players sometimes show their feelings by not singing their country's song. The Australian government tried to help by talking to the players about their choices. Now, some players have gone back to Iran, while others stay in Australia.
Article_Structure: WHO: Three members of Iran's women's soccer team and the Australian government
WHAT: The players decided to return to Iran instead of staying as refugees in Australia
WHY: Concerns about safety in Iran and players given chances to discuss options
